criteria.criterion: The cached YouTube extraction produces a usable Codex prompt and can be replayed without yt-dlp.

customizationQuestion: What should Spanks adapt from this video tutorial to your current project?
extractedSummary: Cached tutorial extraction for the hackathon demo. It treats the video as a Codex workflow: identify the goal, translate the tutorial into a prompt, run Codex, inspect results, and save gotchas.

gotchas.description: Live video extraction can fail if yt-dlp is missing or captions are unavailable.
gotchas.fix: Use the bundled cached extraction so the demo continues smoothly.
gotchas.title: Video extraction needs a fallback

promptSnippets: Convert the tutorial into a practical Codex execution plan.
Ask the user for the target use case before running the generated prompt.
Record any places where the tutorial assumed missing context.

skill.name: youtube-codex-tutorial
skill.title: Turn a YouTube tutorial into a Codex run
skill.yamlContent:
name: youtube-codex-tutorial
steps:
  - title: Load tutorial source
  - title: Extract the core workflow
  - title: Customize the goal
  - title: Run or copy the Codex prompt

steps.description: Load the YouTube tutorial URL and capture the tutorial's goal, tools, and expected output.
steps.gotchaText: If captions or video extraction fail, use the cached skill instead of blocking.
steps.title: Load tutorial source
steps.verification: Pawscript has a title, source URL, and step list.

steps.description: Summarize the tutorial into a small sequence of actions that a human or Codex can execute.
steps.gotchaText: Avoid copying narration verbatim. Extract the durable actions.
steps.title: Extract the workflow
steps.verification: The skill has concrete actions, targets, and verification text.

steps.description: Ask the user what they want to adapt the tutorial for before generating the Codex prompt.
steps.gotchaText: Tutorials often assume one environment; customization keeps the run relevant.
steps.title: Customize the goal
steps.verification: The generated prompt includes the user's current project goal.

steps.description: Run the generated prompt with Codex CLI in ~/Desktop/PawscriptDemo, or copy it for manual Codex UI use.
steps.gotchaText: Keep execution isolated from the Pawscript source repo.
steps.title: Run the Codex prompt
steps.verification: Codex completes or Pawscript saves a gotcha explaining what help was needed.
